seoTitle: Connect and Use Bank Feeds in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Set up live bank feeds in QuickBooks Online to automatically sync transactions instead of manually entering every charge.
intro: Manually entering every debit card swipe is a fast way to burn hours and miss tax deductions. This guide shows how to connect a live bank feed in QuickBooks Online and process the incoming transactions once they start flowing.

steps.heading: Open Bank Transactions
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, to reach your central banking panel.

steps.heading: Link a new account
steps.body: Click Link Account, search for your financial institution, and select it from the results.

steps.heading: Authorize access
steps.body: Enter your bank login credentials in the secure OAuth window to grant API access.

steps.heading: Map your accounts
steps.body: Uncheck any personal profiles, select only business accounts, and map them to the correct line in your Chart of Accounts.

steps.heading: Review and categorize
steps.body: Once transactions populate under For Review, use Match to confirm existing entries or Categorize to assign new expense rows.

faq.question: Is connecting a bank feed secure?
faq.answer: Yes, it uses an encrypted OAuth connection so QuickBooks never stores your raw banking password.

faq.question: Can I pull in older transactions after connecting?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose a historical data retrieval timeframe during setup to fetch past transactions.

faq.question: What's the difference between Match and Categorize?
faq.answer: Match links a downloaded transaction to an entry you already recorded, while Categorize assigns a new transaction to an expense or income account.

faq.question: Can I connect multiple accounts from the same bank?
faq.answer: Yes, during setup you can select multiple checking, savings, or credit card accounts from the same institution.
